title: Lesson Quiz: Types of Investments
description: Assessment of the concept that Stocks, bonds, mutual funds, and retirement accounts.

questions.prompt: Which investment type is most closely connected to company ownership?

choices.feedback: Correct. Stocks are associated with ownership in a company.
Bonds are more closely associated with lending than ownership.
A retirement account is a long-term investment account, not specifically a company ownership type.
A fee schedule is not an investment type.

questions.prompt: A mutual fund is an investment made up of multiple assets.

choices.feedback: A mutual fund does combine multiple assets together.
Correct. Mutual funds are built from multiple investments grouped together.

questions.prompt: Which option is most closely connected to long-term retirement planning?

choices.feedback: This is the strongest match, but the correct answer in this set is placed elsewhere.
A transportation tax is not an investment type.
Correct. Retirement accounts are designed for long-term future planning.
A purchase fee is not an investment type.

questions.prompt: Why is it important to match an investment type to the financial goal?

choices.text: Because all investment types serve the exact same purpose
choices.feedback: Different investment types can fit different goals.

choices.text: Because the strongest investment option is the one that fits the actual goal and timeline
choices.feedback: This is the strongest reason, but the correct answer in this set is placed elsewhere.

choices.text: Because goals should be ignored during investing
choices.feedback: Investment choices are stronger when they are linked to the intended goal.

choices.text: Because the strongest investment option is the one that fits the actual goal and timeline
choices.feedback: Correct. Strong investment decisions match the type of investment to the purpose of the money.

questions.prompt: Choosing an investment without considering the goal is always the strongest strategy.

choices.feedback: Strong investment choices should consider the purpose and goal of the money.
Correct. Matching the investment type to the goal is part of strong financial reasoning.
